semantic_matched_intents.quote: Execute all independent tool calls in parallel when no dependencies exist
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already runs independent tool calls in parallel by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Approach-First Development: Explain approach and get approval before writing code (See Section 7)
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already plans its approach before implementation on non-trivial work by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Multi-File Decomposition: Split work when modifying 3+ files (See Section 7)
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already decomposes non-trivial tasks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Before writing any non-trivial code: Explain the implementation approach clearly Describe which files will be modified and why Get user approval before proceeding
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already plans its approach before implementation on non-trivial work by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: When modifying 3 or more files: Split work into logical units using TodoList Execute changes file-by-file or by logical grouping Analyze file dependencies before parallel execution Report progress after each unit completion
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already decomposes non-trivial tasks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Always prefer Edit tool over sed/awk
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already prefers dedicated tools (like Edit) over Bash when applicable by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: ALWAYS prefer editing existing files over creating new ones
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already prefers editing existing files over creating new ones by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: NEVER proactively create documentation files (*.md) unless explicitly requested
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not write docs, README, or summary files unless explicitly asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never use emojis (no emoji characters).
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not use emojis unless asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No emojis or bracket prefixes (`[CRAWLER]`) in log messages.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not use emojis unless asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Secrets: NEVER commit `.env`
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already never commits secrets or broken code by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No comments on self-evident code; add comments only when logic is non-obvious.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes comments only when the WHY is non-obvious by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No docstrings / JSDoc unless already present in that section.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids multi-paragraph docstrings or multi-line comment blocks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Minimal abstractions: prefer three clear lines over a premature helper function.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids adding abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Comments are crisp: state *why* in one line, not *what* the code already shows.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already writes why-not-what comments by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do not write essays.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids multi-paragraph docstrings or multi-line comment blocks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: A code comment (`//` or `///` `<summary>`) is **one or two lines**.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids multi-paragraph docstrings or multi-line comment blocks by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: If you need a paragraph of rationale, it belongs in the commit message or PR description, not in source.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already avoids multi-paragraph docstrings or multi-line comment blocks in comments and focuses commit messages on WHY by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Never narrate the change ("previously we did X, now Y because...") — that is commit-message content.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already focuses commit messages on WHY by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No premature abstraction — three similar lines are better than a helper used once.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No feature creep — implement only the behavior required now.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Don't `git add` it back.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already never commits secrets by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Subject line (first line, ≤72 chars): one-line summary in active voice.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already uses imperative-mood summaries for commit messages by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Body (after blank line): explain *why* the change is needed, not *what* it changes (the diff shows that).
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already focuses commit messages on WHY not WHAT by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: No accidental files in the diff — `.tj/config.toml`, `.tj-test-data/`, screenshots that were just for debugging, etc.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already never commits secrets or broken code by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: PR body explains the WHY — symptom + root cause + fix, not just "fixes the bug."
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already focuses on WHY not WHAT in commit messages, and this extends the principle to PR bodies.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Do what the brief / issue says, no more.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not add features, refactors, or abstractions beyond what the task requires by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: If you see `.tj/config.toml` in your `git status` as modified or new, that's expected — just don't `git add` it.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already never commits secrets by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Validate before reporting. Check the current codebase state before claiming something is missing. Glob + Grep first.
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already investigates unfamiliar state before deleting or overwriting by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Create documentation files without explicit request
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not write docs, README, or summary files unless explicitly asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Use emojis unless user requests
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already does not use emojis unless asked by default.

semantic_matched_intents.quote: Prefer updating existing pages over creating new ones
semantic_matched_intents.reason: Claude Code already prefers editing existing files over creating new ones by default.
